cards.front: What is a tree data structure?
cards.back: A tree data structure is a hierarchical data structure consisting of nodes, with a root node and child nodes, where each node has a value and zero or more child nodes.
A binary tree is a type of tree data structure in which each node has at most two child nodes.

cards.front: What is the root node of a binary tree?
cards.back: The root node of a binary tree is the topmost node, which has no parent node.

cards.front: What is the difference between a left child and a right child?
cards.back: A left child is the child node located on the left side of the parent node, and a right child is the child node located on the right side of the parent node.

cards.front: What is the purpose of a tree data structure?
cards.back: The primary purpose of a tree data structure is to efficiently store and retrieve data by partitioning it into smaller sub-sets of related data.
